explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: The company references compliance with existing regulations for AI use and demonstrates adherence to external requirements. It acknowledges emerging federal and state regulations for big data and AI, indicating a focus on developing risk management frameworks.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party webcast participation by a Voya Financial leader provides evidence for the **governance** and **external_accountability** pillars of responsible AI. The discussion on building trust and ethics into AI tools, along with compliance with existing regulations for AI use, demonstrates Voya's engagement in establishing AI governance frameworks and adhering to external requirements.
relevant_sources.title: How to build a responsible, compliant, and ethical AI program

relevant_sources.summary: Voya Financial's 2023 10-K filing acknowledges emerging federal and state regulations for big data and AI, supporting **governance** and **external accountability** by indicating a focus on developing risk management frameworks. The filing also touches upon **privacy** and **fairness** by mentioning regulations that protect against discrimination, and demonstrates **transparency** through the description of assumptions used in financial models for fair value determination.
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K Annual Report 2023

fairness.findings: The company's 2023 10-K filing mentions regulations that protect against discrimination, touching upon fairness considerations.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post, "How AI Can Revolutionize Retirement's Hidden Infrastructure," provides evidence for the **governance** pillar. The article describes Voya Financial's implementation of AI for document processing, which implies the necessity of governance and strategic data management for such AI systems.
relevant_sources.title: How AI Can Revolutionize Retirement's Hidden Infrastructure

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: The company mentions the AI's ability to consume data and stay attuned to customer needs. Additionally, the 2023 10-K filing touches upon privacy considerations.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release, "Voya AI Chatbot Success," provides evidence for the **transparency** and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. It supports transparency by describing the AI's capabilities in understanding customer intent and providing an intuitive experience, and by mentioning its data consumption and learning processes. Privacy is inferred from the AI's ability to consume data and stay attuned to customer needs, suggesting a focus on how data is utilized within the AI's operation.
relevant_sources.title: Voya AI Chatbot Success

transparency.findings: The company describes the capabilities of its AI chatbot, including its ability to understand customer intent, provide an intuitive experience, and details its data consumption and learning processes. Additionally, assumptions used in financial models for fair value determination are described.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: Voya Financial's published materials describe an AI chatbot's capabilities in understanding customer intent and providing an intuitive experience, while also referencing regulations that protect against discrimination in relation to fairness. These disclosures address 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with materials also referencing the AI's ability to consume data to stay attuned to customer needs for privacy, and discussing building trust and ethics into AI tools for governance. Drawing from 10 publicly available sources, adherence to external requirements and compliance with existing regulations for AI use are also referenced within external accountability and governance materials. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability or oversight.
